plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk. Fully exit CRWD due to its significant loss and scanner flag. Trim SMH to reduce semiconductor concentration. Add to QQQI for increased income and stability. Increase SPY allocation for broad market exposure and capital preservation.
conservative.read: This mode aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% per month. It significantly reduces risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and high-loss holdings. Expect lower volatility and smaller drawdowns, but also limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SOXL and SPAL to small allocations, reducing leveraged ETF risk. Fully exit CRWD due to its significant loss and scanner flag. Trim SMH to reduce semiconductor concentration. Add to QQQI for increased income and stability. Buy PANW as a quality tech name with a favorable risk/reward. Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This mode targets ~2.5% per month with a risk-first growth approach. It balances broad market exposure with quality tech and reduced leverage. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, aiming for steady growth over time.

plan: Sell CRWD to exit the current high-loss position. Increase SOXL and add TECL to lean into leveraged tech and semiconductor exposure. Add to PANW and buy ASML for high-quality growth in the tech sector. Trim SMH to rebalance overall semiconductor exposure. Maintain a small 5% cash buffer for immediate needs.
aggressive.read: This mode aims for aggressive growth, targeting ~4% per month, by leveraging high-conviction tech and semiconductor plays. It significantly increases exposure to leveraged ETFs, which can lead to larger drawdowns and decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial, as losses can accumulate very rapidly.

plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk. Fully exit CRWD due to its significant loss and scanner flag. Significantly trim SMH to reduce high-beta semiconductor exposure. Maximize QQQI allocation for steady monthly distributions. Increase SPY for broad market stability. Maintain a 15% cash buffer for safety and liquidity.
income.read: This mode prioritizes steady income, targeting ~1% per month, by focusing on income-generating ETFs. It minimizes growth-oriented and leveraged holdings to reduce volatility. While aiming for reliable distributions, NAV can still decline in market downturns, and distributions are not guaranteed.
